create a professional invoice for a registered nurse. PDF if you can. Make it neat, straight to the point. Drop-down menus if possible. This is for a registered nurse working for a home health company. We need to bill them by adding all the services provided on one invoice.

Fixed Provider Information:

Arpi Moradi, RN - permanently displayed on every invoice

Address: 5239 Harter Lane, La Cañada, CA 91101

Phone: 818-515-8980

Email: amoradi2310@gmail.com

✅ Key Features:

Dropdown Service Menu - 12 pre-set nursing services (IV Infusion, Assessments, Wound Care, Medication Management, etc.) with industry-standard rates that auto-populate

Real-Time Calculations - Amounts automatically calculate based on quantity × rate

Add/Remove Rows - Easily add multiple service rows for different patients/services on one invoice

Editable Fields - Customize Bill To company info, invoice number, dates, and rates

Mileage & Other Charges - Add reimbursement amounts that roll into the total

Professional Layout - Healthcare-industry standard design with clear sections

Print to PDF - One-click printing that creates a clean, professional PDF

Signature Lines - Space for RN signature and date

Payment Terms - Editable section for payment instructions and notes

Mobile Responsive - Works perfectly on tablets and desktop

How to Use:

Select a service from the dropdown (rates auto-fill)

Add quantity and adjust rate if needed

Click "Add Service" to add more rows

Enter mileage reimbursement and other charges

Click "Print to PDF" to save or print

The invoice is ready to use immediately—no setup needed!

This application is designed as a **React Single Page Application**. It features a clean, healthcare-professional aesthetic using Tailwind CSS. It supports real-time calculations, dynamic row management, mileage tracking per patient, and native browser PDF generation via a dedicated print stylesheet strategy.

The root cause of "missing info" in PDFs generated from web forms is usually:
1.  **Scrollbars**: Content hidden inside a fixed-height `textarea` or `input` is invisible to the PDF generator.
2.  **Overflow**: Containers with `overflow: hidden` (like the table border) clip content that extends beyond the visible area.
